candidate.name: HTTP/1 pool WrappedSemaphore::try_acquire fast path
candidate.candidate_summary: Add WrappedSemaphore::try_acquire and use it before awaiting acquire in Conns::conn_h1 and HTTP/1 fallback inside conn_negotiate.

refs: Conns::conn_h1 currently awaits self.usable.acquire() before exist_h1_conn
conn_negotiate HTTPS fallback currently awaits self.usable.acquire() before exist_h1_conn
exist_h1_conn requires a WrappedSemPermit before it can return an occupied HTTP/1 Conn
WrappedSemPermit releases the ylong_base semaphore permit on Drop

semantic_assessment.behavior_preserving_shape: A correct implementation would only try to acquire a permit synchronously and fall back to the existing async acquire path when no permit is available.
semantic_assessment.forbidden_shape: Dispatching an HTTP/1 connection before acquiring a permit would weaken max_conn_num semantics and is not allowed.
semantic_assessment.risk: Low correctness risk if implemented as a pure try_acquire fallback; low expected SOTA impact because the measured pool_checkout phase is already microsecond scale.

quantitative_gate.interpretation: Even an impossible 100% elimination of pool_checkout would explain less than 0.32% of nested response_read time in the measured failing cells. This is below the fixed SOTA target and below the threshold for a production change in S9.

counterexample_gate.decision: Do not implement or retain this candidate as S9 production optimization evidence unless future profiling contradicts the current phase data.
